status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.

fields.description: The NM's token secret manager will reject all container tokens after the secret key is activated which means the NM will not launch _any_ containers including AMs.  The whole yarn cluster becomes inoperable in 1d.

resolution.description: A fix for this issue is checked into the tree and tested.
resolution.name: Fixed

fields.summary: RM-NM secret-keys should be randomly generated and rolled every so often

status.description: The issue is considered finished, the resolution is correct. Issues which are not closed can be reopened.
status.name: Closed

comments.body: Exception is InvalidToken "Given Container XXX seems to have an illegally generated token."
Taking a look right now.. Any related logs in the RM and one of the NMs will help.
We had another cluster break after 24h, before the new key should have been activated.  I didn't see anything interesting in the logs, but I'll look again.

Another datapoint on the first cluster that failed after 2d 15m: left the RM up & bounced the NMs.  Cluster worked until RM crossed the 3d mark.  Now it's rejecting all container tokens again.  The 15m activation didn't/shouldn't have occurred so it's more like the second cluster than failed after 1d.
RM appears to be throwing NPE in response to the NM heartbeat.  The NMs never get the new key, so when it's activated, the NMs reject all tokens.
Independent discovery :) I started writing up tests for YARN-39 and immediately ran into this. Have a little patch, cleaning it up now. Will upload it soon.

The bug is in {{ResourceTrackerService}} which was storing _lastKnownMasterKey_ for a node in {{RMNode}} incorrectly. After each update, it was memorizing _nextMasterKey_ which will be reset to null immediately on roll-over. Because of this, NMs get NPEs, don't update their master-keys and hence reject all the containers.

In the attached patch
 - I removed storing _currentKey_ in RMNode altogether. Now NM always reports its _lastKnownMasterKey_ and RM checks if any updates need to be sent.
 - Added {{TestRMNMSecretKeys}} to make sure that RM sends correct key-updates to NM. This was the test which caught the issue for me, it will fail without the code changes and passes after the fix.
 - I also earlier did a bogus fix (without really knowing though :) ) for {{TestContainerManagerSecurity}} over at YARN-39. Corrected that too.

We also need to modify {{NodeStatusUpdaterImpl}} to react properly on exceptions from RM. Will do so separately.

comments.body: Exception is InvalidToken "Given Container XXX seems to have an illegally generated token."
Taking a look right now.. Any related logs in the RM and one of the NMs will help.
We had another cluster break after 24h, before the new key should have been activated.  I didn't see anything interesting in the logs, but I'll look again.

Another datapoint on the first cluster that failed after 2d 15m: left the RM up & bounced the NMs.  Cluster worked until RM crossed the 3d mark.  Now it's rejecting all container tokens again.  The 15m activation didn't/shouldn't have occurred so it's more like the second cluster than failed after 1d.
RM appears to be throwing NPE in response to the NM heartbeat.  The NMs never get the new key, so when it's activated, the NMs reject all tokens.
Independent discovery :) I started writing up tests for YARN-39 and immediately ran into this. Have a little patch, cleaning it up now. Will upload it soon.

The bug is in {{ResourceTrackerService}} which was storing _lastKnownMasterKey_ for a node in {{RMNode}} incorrectly. After each update, it was memorizing _nextMasterKey_ which will be reset to null immediately on roll-over. Because of this, NMs get NPEs, don't update their master-keys and hence reject all the containers.

In the attached patch
 - I removed storing _currentKey_ in RMNode altogether. Now NM always reports its _lastKnownMasterKey_ and RM checks if any updates need to be sent.
 - Added {{TestRMNMSecretKeys}} to make sure that RM sends correct key-updates to NM. This was the test which caught the issue for me, it will fail without the code changes and passes after the fix.
 - I also earlier did a bogus fix (without really knowing though :) ) for {{TestContainerManagerSecurity}} over at YARN-39. Corrected that too.

We also need to modify {{NodeStatusUpdaterImpl}} to react properly on exceptions from RM. Will do so separately.
